paper.title: The highest mass Kepler red giants -- II. Spectroscopic parameters, the amplitude-activity relation, and unexpected halo orbits

extraction.summary: The paper discusses two stars (KIC 11518639 and KIC 8378545) on halo-like orbits with high peculiar velocities, but explicitly frames them as being on high-energy bound orbits resulting from dynamical ejection from multiple systems, not as Galactic-unbound or escaping candidates. No objects are presented as possibly unbound from the Milky Way.

method_chain.summary: Adopted Gaia DR3 astrometry and radial velocities for the sample of 48 high-mass Kepler red giants.

source_refs.context: we used the Gaia DR3 astrometry and radial velocity data along with photogeometric distances to calculate velocity components

method_chain.summary: Used photogeometric distances from Bailer-Jones et al. (2021) based on Gaia DR3 parallax.

source_refs.context: photogeometric distances from Bailer-Jones et al. (2021)

method_chain.summary: Converted sky position, distance, proper motion and radial velocity into Galactocentric velocity components (U, V, W) using astropy.
outputs: Galactocentric velocity components U, V, W

source_refs.context: Used astropy to convert sky position, distance, proper motion and radial velocity into Galactocentric velocity components

method_chain.summary: Integrated orbits forward and calculated orbital parameters (Lz, Etot, eccentricity, zmax) using galpy with a McMillan (2017) Galactic potential.
outputs: orbital parameters: angular momentum, total energy, eccentricity, zmax

source_refs.context: used galpy with a McMillan (2017) Galactic potential to integrate the orbits forward and calculate the orbital parameters

method_chain.summary: Assessed kinematic population membership via Toomre diagram and orbital parameters. The paper identifies two stars on halo-like orbits but characterizes them as high-energy bound orbits, not unbound.

source_refs.context: The two most outlying stars, KIC 11518639 and KIC 8378545, are well outside the thick disk region
Three-body interactions are a common source of hypervelocity stars in the Galaxy and can also place stars on high-energy bound orbits

candidate_groups_considered.description: Two stars in the sample (KIC 11518639 and KIC 8378545) with halo-like kinematics and high peculiar velocities, discussed in the Kinematics and halo orbits section.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: The paper presents these stars as being on high-energy bound orbits resulting from dynamical ejection from multiple systems, not as possibly unbound from the Galaxy. The text explicitly states that three-body interactions can place stars on high-energy bound orbits, distinguishing them from hypervelocity stars.

source_refs.context: Summary of kinematics findings: two stars on halo-like orbits, with ejection from multiple systems as the most likely explanation, and no claim of unbound status
